admin_surface.scope: Top-level DAO. LDO holders vote to authorize protocol changes, treasury actions, parameter updates, contract upgrades. Originating authority for all in-scope governance actions; flows through Dual Governance before execution.

dao._note: 5% of total LDO supply quorum + simple majority of participating tokens. Two-phase voting: 48-72h main phase (yes/no) + 12-24h objection phase (only 'no' votes accepted). Total ~3 days minimum. No proposal threshold — anyone with LDO can theoretically initiate via Aragon UI but practically requires DAO contributor access in current operations.

admin_surface.scope: Aragon Agent app that holds protocol roles, treasury, and executes calls following successful Aragon Vote. Holds DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE on most protocol contracts (StakingRouter, AccountingOracle, ValidatorsExitBusOracle, etc.).

dao._note: Agent itself does not vote — it executes the result of an Aragon Voting decision. Listed separately because it is the on-chain caller for protocol operations, distinct from the voting contract.

admin_surface.scope: Dynamic timelock layer that sits between Aragon Voting decisions and execution. All in-scope governance actions targeting Lido on Ethereum must flow through DG. stETH holders can lock tokens to extend the timelock or trigger Rage Quit (full block until withdrawals complete).

timelock._note: VARIABLE TIMELOCK - schema's delay_seconds field captures only the MINIMUM. Effective delay range: 3 days (Normal state) → 45 days (Veto Signaling at max stETH lock) → unbounded (Rage Quit, until ETH withdrawals complete). When stETH holders lock 1% of TVL, dynamic timelock activates. At 10% locked, Rage Quit triggers. This is not standard timelock semantics. Schema v0.2 candidate: add delay_max_seconds and delay_extensible_by fields.

admin_surface._schema_observation: Dual Governance is a new pattern - stakeholder-extensible timelock. Different from Aave Lvl1/Lvl2 (fixed) or Curve admin_actions_delay (fixed per-pool). The minimum 3-day delay is the score-relevant number; the max bounds can be a secondary score input.

admin_surface.scope: Committee of guardians (typically professional node operators) that signs each batch deposit attestation. Quorum required to ALLOW deposits. ANY single guardian can PAUSE deposits unilaterally (asymmetric thresholds).

multisig._note: Custom committee implemented inside DepositSecurityModule contract, NOT a Gnosis Safe. Asymmetric threshold model: ~2/3 quorum (~4 of 6) required to APPROVE deposits, but ANY 1 guardian can PAUSE via signed message. Initial guardians: Stakefish, Kiln, Blockscape, Staking Facilities, P2P, Lido dev team. Current set requires runtime verification via DSM.getGuardians(). The signer count and threshold can change via DAO vote calling addGuardians/removeGuardians.

admin_surface._schema_observation: Asymmetric multisig threshold (high to allow, low to pause) is a new pattern. Schema's single threshold field doesn't capture this. v0.2 candidate: split into approve_threshold and pause_threshold for committees with asymmetric authority. Or add a separate emergency_controls entry for the unilateral pause path - which is what we'll do here for now.

admin_surface.scope: Multi-member oracle committee that submits daily reports on validator state (balances, exit counts, slashing). Reports drive stETH rebase. Misreporting can affect supply but is sanity-checked on-chain (max APY bounds, max balance drop bounds in OracleReportSanityChecker).

controller._note: Committee membership managed via HashConsensus contract. Quorum required to submit a report. Typically 5-9 members. On-chain sanity checks (max APY, max balance drop) limit blast radius even if committee colludes. Indexer should enumerate via HashConsensus.getMembers() at runtime.

admin_surface.scope: Oracle committee that signals which validators to exit. Post-EIP-7002 (Pectra), the protocol can trigger validator exits using withdrawal credentials without node operator consent. This expands the in-protocol blast radius of the oracle committee.

controller._note: Same membership pattern as Accounting Oracle. EIP-7002 introduces a meaningful operational risk vector: governance-triggered validator exits affect staking yield and ETH withdrawal queue depth, but cannot directly steal funds. Worth surfacing on dashboard.

admin_surface.scope: Optimistic-governance contract that delegates routine operations (node operator additions, reward distributions, allowed recipients) to trusted committees. Motions auto-pass after objection period unless LDO holders veto with 0.5%+ of supply.

controller._note: EVMScriptExecutor is the on-chain caller. Each motion type has its own factory and limits. Out-of-bounds requests fail. Easy Track lowers DAO operational friction but adds attack surface — a malicious motion factory or whitelist update via DAO vote could expand its powers. Indexer should enumerate active motion factories.

upgrade_paths._note: Lido stETH contract is upgradeable via Aragon AppProxyUpgradeable. Admin is Aragon Kernel which delegates to Aragon Voting. Effective delay = 3 days (DG min) + 3 days voting + variable DG extension. Stop-gap: stETH contract has PAUSE_ROLE held by Voting; can stop all transfers, mints, burns.

Most other protocol contracts (StakingRouter, NodeOperatorsRegistry, AccountingOracle, ValidatorsExitBusOracle, WithdrawalQueueERC721) use OpenZeppelin proxy pattern with Agent as ProxyAdmin. Same effective upgrade delay applies.

emergency_controls.scope: Pauses new deposits to validators. ANY single DSM guardian can call. Existing user funds are unaffected; staking continues; withdrawals continue. Defends against deposit-frontrunning attacks where a malicious node operator pre-deposits with their own withdrawal credentials.
emergency_controls._note: Asymmetric power: 1-of-N can pause, ~2/3 quorum required to resume. Deliberately designed for fast defensive response. Pause is time-bounded (pauseIntentValidityPeriodBlocks ≈ 24 hours) requiring re-trigger if needed longer.

emergency_controls.scope: Pauses critical protocol functions (Withdrawal Queue, ValidatorsExitBus, VaultHub) for fixed duration (typically 6-11 days). Designed to give DAO time to vote on a fix. After firing, GateSeal contract loses authority and a new one must be deployed.
emergency_controls._note: ONE-SHOT EMERGENCY MECHANISM. Same time-bounded pattern as Curve's kill_deadline — capability fires once and dies. This pattern is now confirmed across 2 protocols. Functionally analogous to Curve's pattern. The 'can_freeze_user_funds: true' is technically correct (withdrawals are paused) but only temporarily — funds become accessible again automatically when seal expires. Schema v0.2 candidate: distinguish 'temporarily pausable' from 'permanently freezable'.

emergency_controls.scope: Extends an active GateSeal pause to indefinite duration. Only callable when Dual Governance is in non-Normal state (i.e., when normal DAO action is blocked by a Veto Signaling). Multisig with same signer set as GateSeal but higher quorum.
emergency_controls._note: Defends against the case where DG dynamic timelock prevents DAO from acting before GateSeal expires. Composed safety mechanism.

emergency_controls.scope: Halts all stETH operations: transfers, mints, burns, oracle reports, withdrawals. Held only by Aragon Voting via PAUSE_ROLE.
emergency_controls._note: Requires successful DAO vote to invoke. Goes through DG timelock. Most severe pause available; all on-chain Lido operations halt.

protected_actions: all in-scope DAO governance actions targeting Lido on Ethereum protocol contracts
timelocks._note: Emergency Protected Timelock — the Dual Governance execution layer. Min 3 days, max 45 days (dynamic), unbounded if Rage Quit. Has emergency mode capability that can be activated by Emergency Activation Committee (0x8B7854488Fde088d686Ea672B6ba1A5242515f45) but only blocks execution; cannot fast-track.

oracles._note: Lido's own committee-based oracle, not Chainlink. ~5-9 member committee submits daily report via HashConsensus. On-chain sanity checks (OracleReportSanityChecker) bound malicious or buggy reports: max ~10% APY swing per report, max ~5% balance drop. Committee membership and quorum changes require DAO vote → DG timelock.

Separate oracle for validator exit signaling. Post-Pectra (EIP-7002), exit signals can trigger forced validator exits via withdrawal credentials. Material expansion of in-protocol authority over node operators.

meta._out_of_scope_trust_surface: Lido has significant operational trust surface that lives off-chain and is not captured in this schema: (1) Validator signing keys are held by Node Operators on their own infrastructure — Lido cannot directly sign attestations or proposals; if a node operator goes offline, Lido validators stop earning. (2) DSM guardians run a 'lido-council-daemon' off-chain that monitors deposits and signs attestations — daemon downtime stalls deposits but does not freeze funds. (3) Oracle committee members run beacon chain monitoring infrastructure to produce reports. None of these are captured in the on-chain governance schema, but they are real operational risk. The dashboard should include a separate 'off-chain trust surface' section per protocol where applicable.
